file_name: PSGT Agreement NDA New Supplier Non Disclosure Template DC.txt

perturbation.original_text: This CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT (the “Agreement”) is made effective as of the [ ●] day of [ ●], 201[●] by and between Cleary Gottlieb Steen & Hamilton LLP (“CGSH”), a limited liability partnership with registered offices at 2000 Pennsylvania Avenue, NW, Washington, DC 20006 (the “Premises”), and [ ●] (the “Supplier”) with registered office at [ ●] (each a “Party,” and, together, the “Parties”). Fo r the purposes of this Agreement, CGSH includes its wholly owned subsidiaries , as well as its affiliates, branches, and offices both in and outside the United States , and the Premises includes all offices of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: This CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT (the “Agreement”) is made effective as of the [ ●] day of [ ●], 201[●] by and between Cleary Gottlieb Steen & Hamilton LLP (“CGSH”), a limited liability partnership with registered offices at 2000 Pennsylvania Avenue, NW, Washington, DC 20006 (the “Premises”), and [ ●] (the “Vendor”) with registered office at [ ●] (each a “Party,” and, together, the “Parties”). Fo r the purposes of this Agreement, CGSH includes its wholly owned subsidiaries , as well as its affiliates, branches, and offices both in and outside the United States , and the Premises includes all offices of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Supplier' is changed to 'Vendor'. The rest of the document still refers to 'Supplier'. This creates confusion as to which party is bound by the obligations outlined for the 'Supplier', leading to uncertainty about which entity is responsible for the confidentiality obligations outlined later. This contradiction affects the entire document but especially the section where Supplier's duty is mentioned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SUPPLIER’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE A. “Confidential Information” means all confidential, proprietary, or non-public information of the Disclosing Parties (however recorded or preserved) that is disclosed or made available, directly or indirectly, by CGSH or its respective employ ees, officers, representatives or advisors to the Supplier or its affiliates, repres entatives, employees or agents, relating to the business of such Disclosing Party or that otherwis e by its nature would be reasonab ly understood as confidential, whether provided in written or oral form and in any manner whatsoever. Without limiting the foregoing, Confidential Information may include c onfidential or proprietary information owned by a third party and used under license by a Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines one party as the "Supplier," and the contradicted text specifies "SUPPLIER’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE." The changed text replaces "Supplier" with "Vendor." This creates a direct conflict because the section describing the Supplier's duty now refers to a term no longer consistently used throughout the document, creating confusion as to which entity is subject to that duty, thus there is a strong contradiction in text.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of CGSH hiring the Supplier to perform [ ●] services (the “Services”), including as may be set forth in an agreement between the Parties (the “Services Agreement”), and since the Supplier may be provided with or come into contact with confidential and proprietary information belonging to CGSH or its clients (each a “Disclosing Party” and together the “Discl osing Parties”) in connection with its provision of Services, the Supplier hereby agrees as follows: 1.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of CGSH hiring the Supplier to perform [ ●] services (the “Services”), including as may be set forth in an agreement between the Parties (the “Services Agreement”), and since the Service Provider may be provided with or come into contact with confidential and proprietary information belonging to CGSH or its clients (each a “Disclosing Party” and together the “Discl osing Parties”) in connection with its provision of Services, the Supplier hereby agrees as follows: 1.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Supplier' to 'Service Provider' within a sentence where 'Supplier' is also used introduces an in-text contradiction. It becomes unclear which entity is agreeing to the terms of the agreement. This ambiguity undermines the enforceability of the entire clause and affects section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SUPPLIER’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE A. “Confidential Information” means all confidential, proprietary, or non-public information of the Disclosing Parties (however recorded or preserved) that is disclosed or made available, directly or indirectly, by CGSH or its respective employ ees, officers, representatives or advisors to the Supplier or its affiliates, repres entatives, employees or agents, relating to the business of such Disclosing Party or that otherwis e by its nature would be reasonab ly understood as confidential, whether provided in written or oral form and in any manner whatsoever. Without limiting the foregoing, Confidential Information may include c onfidential or proprietary information owned by a third party and used under license by a Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Supplier' to 'Service Provider' in the Changed Text introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which repeatedly refers to 'Supplier' and defines 'Supplier's Duty of Non-Disclosure.' This misalignment of terminology creates an in-text contradiction because it becomes unclear to whom the 'Duty of Non-Disclosure' applies, undermining the enforceability of the clause and aligning with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: SUPPLIER’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE A. “Confidential Information” means all confidential, proprietary, or non-public information of the Disclosing Parties (however recorded or preserved) that is disclosed or made available, directly or indirectly, by CGSH or its respective employ ees, officers, representatives or advisors to the Supplier or its affiliates, repres entatives, employees or agents, relating to the business of such Disclosing Party or that otherwis e by its nature would be reasonab ly understood as confidential, whether provided in written or oral form and in any manner whatsoever. Without limiting the foregoing, Confidential Information may include c onfidential or proprietary information owned by a third party and used under license by a Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE A. “Confidential Information” means all confidential, proprietary, or non-public information of the Disclosing Parties (however recorded or preserved) that is disclosed or made available, directly or indirectly, by CGSH or its respective employ ees, officers, representatives or advisors to the Supplier or its affiliates, repres entatives, employees or agents, relating to the business of such Disclosing Party or that otherwis e by its nature would be reasonab ly understood as confidential, whether provided in written or oral form and in any manner whatsoever. Without limiting the foregoing, Confidential Information may include c onfidential or proprietary information owned by a third party and used under license by a Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'SUPPLIER’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE' to 'CONSULTANT’S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE' in Section 1.A, while the agreement still defines the counterparty as the Supplier, creates confusion. It is unclear to whom this duty now applies, causing a contradiction and rendering the duty potentially unenforceable. This change impacts section 1.B as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Supplier agrees that it may only access and interact with any Confidential Information made available to it to the extent necessary for it to perform the Services. The Supplier further agrees that, as a recipient of Confidential Information: (i) except as expressly permitted by an applicable Services Agreement, it shall not, for any purpose, use, disclose, reproduce, transfer or reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion; (ii) it shall maintain in confidence the Confidential Information and shall, with respect to such Confidential Information, use the same degree of care and exercise reasonable efforts to pr otect such Confidential Information as it uses and exercises with respect to protecting its own si milar confidential and/or proprietary information, but in all events no less than reasonable efforts and care; (iii) it shall only disclose Confidential Information to those of its or its affiliates’ employ ees (a) to whom it is necessary to disclose such Confidential Information for performance of the Se rvices, (b) to whom it has advised of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) who have agreed in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement; (iv) it shall cause the employees to whom the Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to Section 1.B(iii) to comply with the te rms of this Agreement, and it shall be responsible for such compliance and fully liable for any failure to comply; and (v) except as set forth in Section 1.B(iii), it shall not disclose or in any way di sseminate or make available any Confidential Information disclosed or otherwise made available to it or in its possession to any other party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'SUPPLIER'S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE' to 'CONSULTANT'S DUTY OF NON-DISCLOSURE' introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly refers to 'The Supplier' and their obligations regarding Confidential Information. Changing the title to 'CONSULTANT' creates ambiguity and directly contradicts the section that still defines the counterparty and its duties as 'Supplier'. It is unclear whether the Supplier or the Consultant or both are bound by the non-disclosure duty, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Supplier agrees that it may only access and interact with any Confidential Information made available to it to the extent necessary for it to perform the Services. The Supplier further agrees that, as a recipient of Confidential Information: (i) except as expressly permitted by an applicable Services Agreement, it shall not, for any purpose, use, disclose, reproduce, transfer or reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion; (ii) it shall maintain in confidence the Confidential Information and shall, with respect to such Confidential Information, use the same degree of care and exercise reasonable efforts to pr otect such Confidential Information as it uses and exercises with respect to protecting its own si milar confidential and/or proprietary information, but in all events no less than reasonable efforts and care; (iii) it shall only disclose Confidential Information to those of its or its affiliates’ employ ees (a) to whom it is necessary to disclose such Confidential Information for performance of the Se rvices, (b) to whom it has advised of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) who have agreed in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement; (iv) it shall cause the employees to whom the Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to Section 1.B(iii) to comply with the te rms of this Agreement, and it shall be responsible for such compliance and fully liable for any failure to comply; and (v) except as set forth in Section 1.B(iii), it shall not disclose or in any way di sseminate or make available any Confidential Information disclosed or otherwise made available to it or in its possession to any other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Subcontractor agrees that it may only access and interact with any Confidential Information made available to it to the extent necessary for it to perform the Services. The Supplier further agrees that, as a recipient of Confidential Information: (i) except as expressly permitted by an applicable Services Agreement, it shall not, for any purpose, use, disclose, reproduce, transfer or reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion; (ii) it shall maintain in confidence the Confidential Information and shall, with respect to such Confidential Information, use the same degree of care and exercise reasonable efforts to pr otect such Confidential Information as it uses and exercises with respect to protecting its own si milar confidential and/or proprietary information, but in all events no less than reasonable efforts and care; (iii) it shall only disclose Confidential Information to those of its or its affiliates’ employ ees (a) to whom it is necessary to disclose such Confidential Information for performance of the Se rvices, (b) to whom it has advised of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) who have agreed in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement; (iv) it shall cause the employees to whom the Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to Section 1.B(iii) to comply with the te rms of this Agreement, and it shall be responsible for such compliance and fully liable for any failure to comply; and (v) except as set forth in Section 1.B(iii), it shall not disclose or in any way di sseminate or make available any Confidential Information disclosed or otherwise made available to it or in its possession to any other party.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Supplier' is changed to 'Subcontractor' in the beginning of section 1.B, but the rest of the section still refers to 'Supplier'. This introduces a conflict because the initial sentence makes it seem as if the Subcontractor is under these obligations, but the rest of the text makes it seem as if the Supplier is responsible. This will cause issues related to enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Supplier agrees that it may only access and interact with any Confidential Information made available to it to the extent necessary for it to perform the Services. The Supplier further agrees that, as a recipient of Confidential Information: (i) except as expressly permitted by an applicable Services Agreement, it shall not, for any purpose, use, disclose, reproduce, transfer or reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion; (ii) it shall maintain in confidence the Confidential Information and shall, with respect to such Confidential Information, use the same degree of care and exercise reasonable efforts to pr otect such Confidential Information as it uses and exercises with respect to protecting its own si milar confidential and/or proprietary information, but in all events no less than reasonable efforts and care; (iii) it shall only disclose Confidential Information to those of its or its affiliates’ employ ees (a) to whom it is necessary to disclose such Confidential Information for performance of the Se rvices, (b) to whom it has advised of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) who have agreed in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement; (iv) it shall cause the employees to whom the Confidential Information is disclosed pursuant to Section 1.B(iii) to comply with the te rms of this Agreement, and it shall be responsible for such compliance and fully liable for any failure to comply; and (v) except as set forth in Section 1.B(iii), it shall not disclose or in any way di sseminate or make available any Confidential Information disclosed or otherwise made available to it or in its possession to any other party.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Supplier' with 'Subcontractor' at the beginning of section 1.B, while the rest of the section continues to refer to 'Supplier'. The original text consistently refers to the 'Supplier' and their obligations regarding confidential information. The changed text creates a contradiction because the sentence now appears to place obligations on the 'Subcontractor', while the remaining part of the section attributes the same obligations to the 'Supplier', leading to confusion regarding which party is ultimately responsible. This directly conflicts with the original intention and creates ambiguity about the obligations, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Supplier shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it and its affiliates, and their respective representatives, employees a nd agents, and shall take all reasonable measures to prevent such parties fr om using, disclosing, reproducing, transferring, reducing to writing or otherwise recording the Confidential Information as prohibited by this Agreement. If it becomes known to the Supplier t hat (i) it or its affiliates, representatives, employees or agents have breached any of the te rms of this Agreement, or (ii) facts and/or circumstances exist that could be reasonably expect ed to result in disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Supplier shall in each case immedi ately notify CGSH of such breach, facts and/or

If it becomes known to the Supplier t hat (i) it or its affiliates, representatives, employees or agents have breached any of the te rms of this Agreement, or (ii) facts and/or circumstances exist that could be reasonably expect ed to result in disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Supplier shall in each case immedi ately notify CGSH of such breach, facts and/or

This creates a contradiction and ambiguity about who is responsible for the breach of contract and notification requirements. This inconsistency leads to uncertainty about the parties' obligations, affecting the enforceability of this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Supplier shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it and its affiliates, and their respective representatives, employees a nd agents, and shall take all reasonable measures to prevent such parties fr om using, disclosing, reproducing, transferring, reducing to writing or otherwise recording the Confidential Information as prohibited by this Agreement. If it becomes known to the Supplier t hat (i) it or its affiliates, representatives, employees or agents have breached any of the te rms of this Agreement, or (ii) facts and/or circumstances exist that could be reasonably expect ed to result in disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Supplier shall in each case immedi ately notify CGSH of such breach, facts and/or
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'Supplier' consistently, while the changed text replaces 'Supplier' with 'Contractor' at the beginning of the section but continues to use 'Supplier' later in the same section. The contradicted text also refers to the 'Supplier'. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding who is responsible for the breach of contract and notification requirements. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reas on or at the written request of a Disclosing Party at any time, the Supplier shall destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction) or promptly deliver to each Disclosing Party, or to the requesting Disclosing Party as the case may be, all tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession, including but no t limited to copies, notes, packages, pictures, diagrams, computer memory media and all other tangible embodiments of, or materials containing, any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reas on or at the written request of a Disclosing Party at any time, the Company shall destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction) or promptly deliver to each Disclosing Party, or to the requesting Disclosing Party as the case may be, all tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession, including but no t limited to copies, notes, packages, pictures, diagrams, computer memory media and all other tangible embodiments of, or materials containing, any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Supplier' to 'Company' in the beginning of section 1.F, while the rest of the section still refers to 'Supplier'. This creates a contradiction and ambiguity about who is responsible for the termination of service and on whose request the Disclosing party is asking the service. This inconsistency leads to uncertainty about the parties' obligations, affecting the enforceability of this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the termination of the Services for any reas on or at the written request of a Disclosing Party at any time, the Company shall destroy (and promptly deliver a certification of such destruction) or promptly deliver to each Disclosing Party, or to the requesting Disclosing Party as the case may be, all tangible embodiments of such Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in the Supplier’s or its employees’ possession, including but no t limited to copies, notes, packages, pictures, diagrams, computer memory media and all other tangible embodiments of, or materials containing, any portion of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Supplier' with 'Company' at the beginning of the section, while the contradicted text, which is the same section, retains 'Supplier' in reference to the obligations. This creates a contradiction because the section refers to both 'Company' and 'Supplier', leading to confusion about which entity is responsible for fulfilling the stated obligations regarding the destruction or delivery of confidential information. This misalignment of terminology introduces ambiguity and a direct conflict, thereby creating an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
